title: S-Equiv target-response equivalence contract
description: Specifies equality of target-relevant response for a declared context class, with type data deciding compatible contexts and local tests representing T. The record operationalizes the S-Equiv response-equivalence lemma schema in the structural exhaustion framework (branch_closure_methodology_extended.tex, "Lemma schemas").

schema.description: Required contract discriminator; it must be the literal `S-Equiv`.

left.description: Required statement of the first response, representation, support, quotient state, or predicate to be compared.

informal.description: Required nonempty prose definition of the left-hand object or response, including its boundary/interface data.

latex.description: Optional LaTeX rendering of the left-hand statement, using notation declared in V and L.

formalLanguage.description: Optional name and version of the formal language used for the left-hand encoding.

formal.description: Optional machine-checkable encoding of the left-hand statement.

right.description: Required statement of the second response, representation, support, quotient state, or predicate claimed to agree with `left`.

informal.description: Required nonempty prose definition of the right-hand object or response, including the same declared interface as the left-hand side.

latex.description: Optional LaTeX rendering of the right-hand statement.

formalLanguage.description: Optional name and version of the formal language used for the right-hand encoding.

formal.description: Optional machine-checkable encoding of the right-hand statement.

contextClass.description: Required statement of every compatible context over which equivalence is asserted, using the context classes, interfaces, external types, and local tests recorded in V rather than contexts seen only in one branch.

informal.description: Required nonempty prose definition of the context class, including label, gluing, degree, and admissibility restrictions.

latex.description: Optional LaTeX rendering of the context class, for example a quantified class of `T`-boundaried graphs.

formalLanguage.description: Optional name and version of the formal language used to encode the context class.

formal.description: Optional machine-checkable predicate defining exactly the same context class.

responseCriterion.description: Required biconditional defining equal response. It must require agreement of the target predicate T after every permitted context operation and preserve all interface coordinates, exact types, and local-test outcomes named by V.

informal.description: Required nonempty prose statement of the all-context response biconditional and all interface coordinates that must be preserved.

latex.description: Optional LaTeX rendering of the response criterion.

formalLanguage.description: Optional name and version of the formal language used for the criterion.

formal.description: Optional machine-checkable formulation of the all-context response equivalence.

directionsProved.description: Optional explicit direction checklist. When present for a genuine equivalence it must contain both unique values `left_to_right` and `right_to_left`, documenting both implications of the response biconditional.

items.description: One proved implication direction, using the schema's fixed direction name.

evidence.description: Required nonempty list of sources establishing the response equality, including all-context quantification and preservation of any boundary degree profile.

items.description: One evidence record supporting the equivalence.

kind.description: Required evidence category from the fixed evidence-kind alphabet.

reference.description: Required nonempty locator for a definition, S-Def record, S-Equiv proof, finite context generator, exact-type table, local-test equivalence theorem, or formal proof.

hash.description: Optional integrity hash for a finite table, program, or formal proof artifact.

notes.description: Optional note identifying the two implications and explaining how interface preservation is checked.
